bug_locations.reason: The separator matching regex requires a preceding character before the separator, so inputs where the separator is at position 0 or follows an escape boundary can be parsed incorrectly. This likely breaks escaped long separator handling tested in test_escape_longsep.

Escaped separator replacement iterates separators in declaration order, causing shorter separators like ':' to be unescaped before longer ones like ':='. This can corrupt parsing of escaped long separators such as '\:='.
